Keywords: nodejs, node, javascript runtime, v8, server-side js
Description: JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine; enables server-side JavaScript execution with an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model.
When To Use: Run JavaScript outside the browser, build APIs, CLI tools, and real-time services.
Output: Executes the script and streams stdout/stderr to the terminal.

Keywords: npm, node package manager, install, registry
Description: Default package manager bundled with Node.js; installs dependencies from the npm registry and manages project scripts.
When To Use: Install project dependencies, run lifecycle scripts, and publish packages to the npm registry.
Output: Creates or updates node_modules/ and generates package-lock.json.

Keywords: pnpm, fast package manager, content-addressable, disk efficient
Description: Fast, disk-efficient package manager that uses a content-addressable store and hard links to avoid duplicating packages across projects.
When To Use: Manage dependencies in projects or monorepos where disk space and install speed matter.
Output: Creates node_modules/ using symlinks and a global content-addressable store; generates pnpm-lock.yaml.

Keywords: bun, fast runtime, bundler, all-in-one, zig
Description: All-in-one JavaScript/TypeScript runtime, package manager, bundler, and test runner built from scratch in Zig for maximum speed.
When To Use: Replace Node.js + npm + bundler in projects that benefit from faster installs, faster startup, and native TypeScript support.
Output: Executes scripts or installs dependencies significantly faster than traditional tooling.

Keywords: tsc, typescript, type checking, compilation, tsconfig
Description: Official TypeScript compiler; performs static type checking and transpiles .ts/.tsx files to JavaScript.
When To Use: Type-check your codebase during CI or before a release; compile TypeScript to JavaScript when not using a bundler.
Output: Reports type errors to stderr or emits .js/.d.ts files into the configured outDir.

Keywords: tsx, typescript execute, esm, no compile, fast
Description: TypeScript execution engine that runs .ts/.tsx files directly without a separate compilation step, powered by esbuild.
When To Use: Quickly run TypeScript files during development without setting up a full build pipeline.
Output: Executes the TypeScript file immediately and streams stdout/stderr.

Keywords: ts-node, typescript node, register, repl, swc
Description: TypeScript execution environment for Node.js; hooks into Node's module system to compile TypeScript on the fly.
When To Use: Run TypeScript in Node.js when you need full tsconfig respect and type-checking at runtime.
Output: Compiles and executes TypeScript in-process; outputs results to stdout/stderr.

Keywords: eslint, linter, static analysis, code quality, rules
Description: Pluggable JavaScript/TypeScript linter that statically analyzes code to find problems, enforce style, and prevent bugs.
When To Use: Catch bugs, enforce coding conventions, and auto-fix fixable issues across the codebase.
Output: Prints warnings and errors to stdout; returns a non-zero exit code if errors remain.

Keywords: prettier, formatter, code style, opinionated, auto format
Description: Opinionated code formatter that enforces a consistent style by parsing code and reprinting it with its own rules.
When To Use: Format code automatically so the team never debates style; integrate into pre-commit hooks or CI.
Output: Rewrites files in place and prints the list of changed files to stdout.

Keywords: vitest, test runner, vite, fast tests, watch mode
Description: Vite-powered test framework with native ESM support, TypeScript out of the box, and a Jest-compatible API.
When To Use: Run unit and integration tests in Vite-based projects or any project that benefits from fast HMR-style test re-runs.
Output: Prints test results (pass/fail/skip) to stdout and returns a non-zero exit code on failure.

Keywords: jest, testing framework, snapshot, mock, coverage
Description: Batteries-included JavaScript testing framework with built-in assertions, mocking, snapshot testing, and code coverage.
When To Use: Write and run unit/integration tests, generate coverage reports, and use snapshot testing for UI components.
Output: Prints test results and optional coverage summary to stdout; generates coverage/ directory.

Keywords: nvm, node version manager, switch node, .nvmrc
Description: Node.js version manager that lets you install and switch between multiple Node.js versions per shell session.
When To Use: Switch Node.js versions across projects that require different runtimes; ensure the team uses the same version via .nvmrc.
Output: Activates the specified Node.js version in the current shell session.

Keywords: clinicjs, clinic, profiling, performance, flamegraph, doctor
Description: Performance profiling suite for Node.js that auto-diagnoses issues using Doctor, Bubbleprof, and Flame sub-commands.
When To Use: Profile a slow Node.js application to identify event-loop delays, I/O bottlenecks, or hot code paths.
Output: Generates an interactive HTML report with flamegraphs and performance recommendations.

Keywords: esbuild, bundler, minifier, fast build, go
Description: Ultra-fast JavaScript/TypeScript bundler and minifier written in Go; orders of magnitude faster than traditional bundlers.
When To Use: Bundle and minify code when build speed is critical, such as in large codebases or rapid CI pipelines.
Output: Produces bundled/minified output files in the specified directory.

Keywords: tsup, typescript bundler, library build, dts, esbuild
Description: Zero-config TypeScript library bundler built on esbuild; generates ESM, CJS, and .d.ts outputs with minimal configuration.
When To Use: cjs --dts
Output: Build and publish TypeScript libraries that need to ship ESM, CJS, and type declarations.
column9: Emits bundled .js and .d.ts files into the dist/ directory.

Keywords: turborepo, turbo, monorepo, build orchestration, cache, pipeline
Description: High-performance monorepo build system that caches task outputs and parallelizes work across packages.
When To Use: Orchestrate builds, tests, and linting across a monorepo with intelligent caching and task scheduling.
Output: Runs tasks in topological order across workspaces; prints results and cache hit/miss info.

Keywords: playwright, e2e, end to end, browser testing, cross-browser, automation
Description: Cross-browser end-to-end testing framework by Microsoft; drives Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit with a single API.
When To Use: Write reliable E2E tests that run across all major browsers; capture screenshots, traces, and video on failure.
Output: Prints test results to stdout and generates an HTML report in the playwright-report/ directory.

Keywords: prisma, orm, database, migration, schema, generate
Description: Database toolkit CLI that manages schema migrations, generates a type-safe client, and introspects existing databases.
When To Use: Define database schemas, create and apply migrations, and generate a type-safe database client.
Output: Applies migrations to the database and regenerates the Prisma Client in node_modules/.prisma/client.

Keywords: docker, container, image, dockerfile, devops, deploy
Description: Platform for building, shipping, and running applications in isolated containers, ensuring consistent environments.
Command: docker build -t myapp . && docker run -p 3000:3000 myapp
When To Use: Package a Node.js application with its exact runtime environment for consistent deployment across dev, staging, and production.
Output: Builds a container image and/or starts a container that exposes the application on the mapped port.

Keywords: nodemon, auto restart, watch, file changes, dev server
Description: Utility that monitors file changes in a Node.js project and automatically restarts the server during development.
When To Use: Auto-restart the development server whenever source files change, eliminating manual restart cycles.
Output: Watches the file system and restarts the process on change; prints restart notifications to stdout.

Keywords: husky, lint-staged, git hooks, pre-commit, quality gate
Description: Combination of husky (Git hook manager) and lint-staged (run linters on staged files only) to enforce code quality before every commit.
Command: npx husky init && npm pkg set scripts.pre-commit=""npx lint-staged"""
When To Use: Prevent bad code from entering the repository by auto-running linters, formatters, and tests on staged files before commit.
Output: Runs configured tasks on staged files during the pre-commit hook; blocks the commit if any task fails.

Keywords: knip, unused code, unused dependencies, dead code, cleanup
Description: Tool that finds unused files, exports, dependencies, and devDependencies in JavaScript/TypeScript projects.
When To Use: Audit a project for dead code and unused dependencies before releases or during periodic cleanup.
Output: Prints a report of unused files
